interactions.mechanism: Vitamin K counteracts warfarin's anticoagulant effect.
interactions.advice: KEEP vitamin K intake CONSISTENT day-to-day. Do NOT suddenly eat large amounts of green leafy vegetables.
interactions.danger: Sudden increase → blood clots. Sudden decrease → excessive bleeding.

interactions.mechanism: Grapefruit inhibits CYP3A4 enzyme, causing statin blood levels to rise dangerously.
interactions.advice: COMPLETELY AVOID grapefruit and grapefruit juice while on statins.
interactions.danger: Can cause rhabdomyolysis (muscle breakdown), kidney failure.

interactions.mechanism: Tyramine + MAOI → uncontrolled norepinephrine release → hypertensive crisis.
interactions.advice: COMPLETELY AVOID tyramine-rich foods. Can cause a potentially fatal hypertensive crisis.
interactions.danger: Sudden severe headache, rapid heart rate, stroke, death.

interactions.mechanism: Alcohol + metformin → increased lactic acidosis risk.
interactions.advice: AVOID or strictly limit alcohol while on metformin.
interactions.danger: Lactic acidosis can be fatal. Also worsens blood sugar control.

interactions.mechanism: ACE inhibitors raise blood potassium. High-K foods → hyperkalemia.
interactions.advice: LIMIT high-potassium foods. Avoid potassium-based salt substitutes.

interactions.mechanism: These foods reduce thyroid medication absorption.
interactions.advice: Take thyroid medication on empty stomach, 30-60 min before these foods.
interactions.danger: Reduced medication effectiveness → hypothyroid symptoms return.

interactions.mechanism: Sodium and caffeine changes alter lithium blood levels unpredictably.
interactions.advice: Keep sodium and caffeine intake CONSISTENT. Do not drastically change diet.
interactions.danger: Low sodium → lithium toxicity. High sodium → lithium ineffectiveness.

interactions.mechanism: Grapefruit increases drug blood levels via CYP3A4 inhibition.
interactions.danger: Excessive blood pressure drop, dizziness, fainting.

interactions.mechanism: Minerals chelate with tetracyclines, blocking absorption.
interactions.advice: Take antibiotic 1 hour before or 2 hours after dairy/minerals.

interactions.mechanism: These foods/supplements have natural antiplatelet effects, compounding the drug effect.
interactions.advice: Moderate intake. Report any unusual bruising or bleeding to doctor.
interactions.danger: Excessive bleeding risk from compounded antiplatelet effects.

interactions.mechanism: Drug retains potassium + high-K food → dangerous hyperkalemia.
interactions.advice: LIMIT high-potassium foods. Monitor blood potassium regularly.
